title: 2 Terminology
body_text:
2	Terminology
2.1	Definitions
Clearing
Clearing means the process of exchanging and reconciling payment items that result in a transfer of funds from one financial institution to another.
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1âs applications and to information and news about our products and services.
Completed Money Orders File
The Completed Money Orders File is the credit unionâs file of cashed Money Orders.
CPA
CPA means Canadian Payments Association, also known as Payments Canada.
Money Order
Money Orders are payment items issued by the credit union for the purpose of ordering a payment to be made to a payee.
ORS
Online Return System (ORS) is a web-based system that facilitates the return of cheques and allows users to research deposit items and reinstate cheques. ORS also facilitates the return of AFT Transactions and the creation of AFT Notice of Change transactions.
Outstanding Money Orders File
The Outstanding Money Orders File is the credit unionâs file of issued but uncashed Money Orders.
PCMLTFA
The
Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act (Canada)
is an
Act
to facilitate combatting the laundering of proceeds of crime and the financing of terrorist activities.
Signature Card
A Signature Card as any record that is signed by an individual who is authorized to give instructions in respect of the account.
END
